results.possibleIdentity: John Grippe Junior (likely the individual identified as 'Hohn Grippe Junior' on the historical index card) was a technical professional and engineer active in Queens, New York, during the late 20th century. Born around 1949, he had a career spanning over four decades as an estimator for A.J. McNulty & Co., a construction engineering firm based in Maspeth, Queens, specializing in structural steel and precast concrete. This professional background aligns with the technical field of 'constructing engineering sciences' noted in his historical records. Grippe was likely a participant or technical volunteer for Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) in the late 1960s or early 1970s, as the organization frequently collaborated with engineers in the New York City area to support artistic projects. Beyond his professional life, he was an avid chess player, serving as the president of the Maspeth Chess Club, and was a cantor at the Holy Cross Ukrainian Church in Astoria, Queens. He resided in the Queens neighborhoods of Maspeth and Astoria and died in 2024 at the age of 75.
